_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, scorecard-refine-local cron 2026-06-05 0030)
_meta.note: LOCAL batch (5): Denver Mayor (CO) + DC Council/AG (At-Large Bonds, AG Schwalb, Ward 1 Nadeau, Ward 2 Pinto). Evidence-based finalization with cited URLs. PRIORITY CATCHES: (1) Brooke Pinto = MAIN-STREET-PUBLIC-SAFETY-DEMOCRAT — authored Secure DC Omnibus 2024 with FOUR HARD-TRUE public_justice cells (pretrial detention rebuttable presumption, MPD funding floor, minority push for higher gun penalties during RCCA, expanded firearm felonies); social/life cells still uniformly FALSE. (2) Anita Bonds = ORTHODOX-D-WITH-FISCAL-DEVIATION — one of only 5 NO votes on 2021 DC millionaire tax (econ q2 HARD-TRUE), plus Secure DC YES 'monumental' (public_justice q4 HARD-TRUE). (3) Mike Johnston = SCHOOL-CHOICE + PRO-LE D — public Montview Presbyterian member, endorsed pro-charter DPS board slate, 'we have to give law enforcement the tools'; but heavy FALSE on guns/sanctuary/abortion/Pride. (4) Brian Schwalb = ORTHODOX BLUE-STATE AG — uniform FALSE on substance defended (sanctuary, cashless bail, mag ban, mifepristone, noncitizen voting); Home Rule litigation is federal not state axis → null on refuse_state_overreach. (5) Brianne Nadeau = BOTTOM-DECILE PROGRESSIVE LAME-DUCK — author of noncitizen voting + Human Rights Sanctuary; announced Sept 25 2025 will not seek 4th term (term ends Jan 2 2027). RUBRIC INTERPRETATION: refuse_state_overreach left NULL for ALL FOUR DC officials — DC has no state-overreach axis (Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17, not a state); cells don't cleanly apply. Same for Johnston where flagged anti-overreach posture is anti-federal not anti-state. HIGH-BAR public_justice q0 applied: Pinto + Johnston earn TRUE via affirmative pro-LE advocacy/programs they run; Bonds left NULL despite Secure DC YES (no clean affirmative pro-MPD statement).

profile.religion: Montview Boulevard Presbyterian Church, Denver (PCUSA, member; published 'faith in the public square' piece Nov 3 2025)
profile.background: 46th Mayor of Denver, sworn in July 17 2023 (term ends July 19 2027). Former Colorado state senator; education-policy background.

mike-johnston.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (denvergov.org/Government/Agencies-Departments-Offices/.../Mayors-Office). HARD evidence: (life) campaign pledges to vigorously implement Colorado SB23-188 abortion-shield law and build a city repro-care portal, Denver joined Planned Parenthood funding amicus Oct 15 2025; (marriage) House1000 includes 'Elati Village — Denver's only micro-community for transgender people' (city-funded trans housing), endorsed Denver bid for 2030 Gay Games, posted 'Happy Pride, Denver!' from Mayor account June 2023; (family q0) endorsed pro-charter DPS school-board slate (Sia/Youngquist/Delarosa) 2025 + documented pro-charter record (HARD-TRUE — school-choice cross-party catch); (christian_liberty q0) Montview Presbyterian member, PCUSA piece (SOFT-TRUE — sincere Christian profession in mainline frame); (christian_liberty q3) convenes quarterly interfaith leader coalition incl. Catholic parish town hall (SOFT-TRUE — broad-faith, includes Christian); (econ q2) sponsored 2024 Ballot Issue 2R (0.5% sales-tax hike to 9.31% for housing, narrowly defeated 50.74-49.26%) + championed Nov 2025 Vibrant Denver bond ~$950M new long-term debt (~$1.8B over life); (public_justice q0) HARD-TRUE — 'If we expect safer streets, we have to give our law enforcement the tools to deliver them' + launched Hot Streets DPD patrol initiative (HIGH-BAR met via affirmative program), but Feb 26 2026 EO 152 directs DPD/sheriff to 'intervene' against federal ICE officers (asterisk); (public_justice q1) FALSE — March 2026 Denver Council reduce-max-sentences proposal under his admin + Roads to Recovery diversion-first; (public_justice q3) FALSE — explicitly 'I see street homelessness as absolutely a public-health issue' Aug 2025; (border q1) 1-hour ICE notice on Abraham Gonzalez (Tren de Aragua) release + $350M migrant services for 42,000 arrivals 2023-24 + faith-based duty framing; (border q2) EO 152 Feb 26 2026 bars ICE from city property without judicial warrant, bars DHS data-sharing, directs DPD to intervene against federal immigration officers; (self_defense) candidate pledge to enforce CO red-flag + 10-day Denver waiting period (q0 FALSE) + May 4 2026 'hell no... Denver will not be bullied' defending Denver's AWB & >15-round mag ban against DOJ (q1 FALSE). NULL: election integrity (no public position on hand-counted/voter-ID/Zuckerbucks); family q1/q2 (mayor lacks direct DPS authority and no statement); christian_liberty q2/q4; public_justice q2/q4; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (anti-overreach posture is anti-federal, not vs Colorado state mandates).

0.note: Campaign pledges to vigorously implement Colorado SB23-188 abortion shield + Denver Public Health repro-care portal; city joined Planned Parenthood funding amicus Oct 15 2025.

1.note: Actively expands abortion-access infrastructure; opposite of abolition.

2.note: House1000 initiative includes Elati Village — 'Denver's only micro-community for transgender people' (city-funded trans-affirming housing).

4.note: Endorsed Denver bid for 2030 Gay Games; posted 'Happy Pride, Denver!' from Mayor account; runs trans micro-community as official policy.

0.note: Documented record supporting school choice/charters; endorsed pro-charter DPS board slate (Sia/Youngquist/Delarosa) 2025 — cross-party catch.
Publicly identifies as Christian, member of Montview Boulevard Presbyterian Church; PCUSA published his 'faith in the public square' piece Nov 3 2025.

3.note: Convenes quarterly interfaith leader coalition; appeared at Catholic parish town hall (broad-faith engagement that includes Christianity in the public square).

2.note: Sponsored 2024 Ballot 2R sales-tax hike (rejected); championed Nov 2025 Vibrant Denver bond ~$950M new long-term debt (~$1.8B over life).

0.note: HIGH-BAR met: affirmative pro-LE advocacy + launched Hot Streets DPD patrol initiative. Quoted: 'If we expect safer streets, we have to give our law enforcement the tools to deliver them.' (Note: Feb 2026 ICE-intervention EO complicates but does not negate the pro-MPD record.)

1.note: March 2026 Denver Council proposal under his admin to reduce max sentences for low-level charges; Roads to Recovery emphasizes diversion-over-prosecution.

3.note: Explicitly framed: 'I see street homelessness as absolutely a public health issue' — public-health framing of street disorder/crime.

1.note: Denver gave only 1-hour notice to ICE on Tren de Aragua suspect Abraham Gonzalez's release; spent $350M+ on migrant services for 42,000 arrivals 2023-24.

2.note: EO 152 (Feb 26 2026): bars ICE from city property without judicial warrant, bars DHS data-sharing, directs DPD/sheriff to intervene against federal immigration officers; schools/churches/hospitals/stadiums off-limits.

0.note: As candidate pledged to enforce CO red-flag law and push 10-day waiting period for Denver gun purchases.

1.note: May 4 2026: 'Hell no... Denver will not be bullied' — publicly defended Denver's assault-weapons & >15-round magazine ban against DOJ demand; pledged enforcement of CO red-flag law.

profile.background: DC Council At-Large since Dec 11 2012; former DC Democratic Party chair (2006-2018); long career in Marion Barry's orbit; chairs Housing committee. Elected Council Chair pro tempore Jan 20 2026 (succeeded Kenyan McDuffie). Announced Dec 7 2025 will NOT seek 2026 reelection — serves through current term (Jan 2027).

anita-bonds.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (dccouncil.gov/council/anita-bonds). Mainstream institutional DC Democrat — uniformly progressive on abortion/LGBTQ/sanctuary/noncitizen-voting (HARD-FALSE Pillar A + immigration). TWO cross-pressure HARD-TRUEs worth noting: (1) **economic_stewardship q2 HARD-TRUE** — one of only 5 NO votes against the 2021 amendment to FY2022 Budget Support Act raising taxes on residents earning >$250K (8-5 vote July 20 2021), framing protection of middle-income property/bond-holders; (2) **public_justice q4 HARD-TRUE** — voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus 2024 (B25-0345) expanding police authority, drug-free zones, pretrial detention: 'This bill is monumental in its attempt to make us all feel safer.' Also (public_justice q2 SOFT-TRUE) — introduced (later withdrawn) Secure DC amendment expanding 'crime of violence' definition for senior victims. Pillar A: HARD-FALSE life q0/q1 — co-introduced D.C. Law 23-90 Reproductive Health Protections (2020) AND D.C. Law 24-254 Enhanced Reproductive Health Protections (2022) shielding self-managed abortion and providers. HARD-FALSE marriage q4 — cosponsored B25-0298 Black LGBTQ History Preservation Establishment Amendment Act mandating LGBTQIA+ Affairs research/report. Election integrity q1 FALSE (SOFT) — Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act 24-242 (noncitizen voting in DC local elections) passed Oct 2022 on near-unanimous Council votes; no record of Bonds dissenting and she chairs Council Operations w/ election focus. Public_justice q1 FALSE (SOFT) — backed RCCA veto override 12-1 (Jan 2023), called it 'very good bill.' Border q1/q2 FALSE (HARD) — voted YES Sanctuary Values Amendment Act of 2020 (D.C. Law 23-282, 13-0). NULL: christian_liberty all 4 (no public profession of Christ on record); marriage q0/q2 (only SOFT third-party characterizations, no own quote); family_child_sovereignty q0/q1/q2 (DC OSP is federally funded, no Bonds vote/statement located); election q0/q4; public_justice q0 (no clean 'back the blue' statement — HIGH BAR not met) and q3; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17); self_defense q0/q1 (no specific Bonds vote located on the 2018 ERPO/red-flag or AWB).

0.note: Co-introduced Strengthening Reproductive Health Protections Amendment Act (D.C. Law 23-90, 2020) codifying right to abortion in DC law.

1.note: Co-introduced Enhancing Reproductive Health Protections Amendment Act (D.C. Law 24-254, 2022) shielding self-managed abortion and out-of-state providers.

4.note: Cosponsored Black LGBTQ History Preservation Establishment Amendment Act 2024 (B25-0298) mandating LGBTQIA+ Affairs research/report.

2.note: HEADLINE CROSS-PRESSURE: one of only 5 NO votes against amending FY2022 Budget Support Act to raise taxes on residents earning >$250K (8-5 vote, July 20 2021).

1.note: Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 (D.C. Law 24-242) allowing noncitizens to vote in DC local elections; Bonds was part of the unanimous Council majority that passed it Oct 2022.
Supported the RCCA which kept DC's no-cash-bail framework; voted YES on the Jan 2023 12-1 veto override, calling it 'a very good bill.'

2.note: Introduced (later withdrawn) Secure DC amendment expanding 'crime of violence' definition to cover trespassing/coercion/unlawful entry against senior victims.

4.note: Voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus Amendment Act (B25-0345) expanding police authority, drug-free zones, pretrial detention. Quoted: 'This bill is monumental in its attempt to make us all feel safer.'

1.note: Voted YES on Sanctuary Values Amendment Act (D.C. Law 23-282, 13-0 unanimous Dec 1 & 15 2020) — bars DC police cooperation with ICE absent judicial warrant.

2.note: Sanctuary Values vote plus support for noncitizen-voting Local Resident Voting Rights Act 2022.

profile.background: DC Attorney General, sworn in Jan 2 2023. Launched re-election campaign Sept 3 2025 for Nov 3 2026 general. Pre-AG: Venable LLP partner (tax/litigation); UVA Law.

brian-schwalb.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (oag.dc.gov). Classic blue-state-AG profile — uniformly progressive on substance, with the additional wrinkle that as DC AG every 'federal overreach' lawsuit (sanctuary, MPD takeover, National Guard removal, tax-cut override) is procedurally TRUE-direction on a Home-Rule axis but SUBSTANTIVELY FALSE on the rubric (because what he's defending is sanctuary, cashless bail, noncitizen voting, gun control). Score against substance per rubric directions. HARD-FALSE: (life q1) joined 23-AG mifepristone amicus in *Alliance for Hippocratic Medicine v. FDA* + co-led 18-AG *Bryant v. Stein* mifepristone coalition + sued Easy Healthcare to protect reproductive data; (family q1) joined CA-led 16-AG amicus defending Chico Unified school policy withholding gender-identity info from parents; (election q1) defended DC's noncitizen-voting Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 in litigation through DC Circuit (June 2025 reversal for standing); (public_justice q1) joint statement w/ Mayor Bowser & Chair Mendelson opposing Congressional bills to end DC cashless bail — 'cash bail creates a two-tiered justice system'; (public_justice q3) OAG routinely frames youth crime via 'trauma, behavioral health support, mentoring, family therapy' through ACE diversion program — explicit public-health rehabilitation paradigm; (border q1) rejected AG Bondi's order requiring DC police cooperation with ICE as 'unlawful'; (border q2) OAG operates 'Defending Our Immigrant Community' program + Jan 24 2025 guidance restricting ICE access to DC schools; (self_defense q0) led 18-AG coalition defending Maryland sensitive-places carry restrictions in *Kipke/Novotny v. Moore* + defends DC firearms-on-transit ban; (self_defense q1) defends DC large-capacity-magazine prohibition + co-led 24-AG VanDerStok ghost-gun amicus (7-2 upheld March 26 2025). NULL: life q0; marriage q0/q2/q4 (only indirect coalition signal, no DC-issued release on Skrmetti specifically); family q0/q2; christian_liberty all 4 (no record on 303 Creative/Masterpiece Cakeshop/FFRF — the 'Brian L. Schwalb' on those filings is unrelated counsel); economic_stewardship q2; election q0/q4; public_justice q0 (Home-Rule defense of MPD chief is SOFT, not HIGH-BAR pro-LE), q2, q4 (mixed sword/diversion); refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — his Home Rule litigation is anti-federal, not anti-state).

1.note: Joined 23-AG mifepristone amicus in Alliance for Hippocratic Medicine v. FDA (April 2023); co-led 18-AG Bryant v. Stein NC mifepristone coalition.
DC joined 16-AG coalition (CA-led) defending Chico Unified school policy withholding gender-identity info from parents.
Defended DC's Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 (allows non-citizens with 30-day residency to vote in local elections) in litigation through DC Circuit June 2025.

Joint statement w/ Mayor Bowser & Chair Mendelson opposing Congressional bills to end DC cashless bail: 'Cash bail creates a two-tiered justice system where poor people stay locked up simply because they cannot afford bail.'

3.note: OAG routinely frames youth crime via 'trauma, behavioral health support, mentoring, family therapy' through ACE diversion program — explicit public-health rehabilitation paradigm.

1.note: Rejected AG Bondi's order requiring DC police cooperation with ICE as 'unlawful'; OAG actively defends DC sanctuary law forbidding MPD assistance to federal immigration absent judicial warrant.

2.note: OAG operates 'Defending Our Immigrant Community' program; Jan 24 2025 guidance restricting ICE access to DC schools.

0.note: Led 18-AG coalition defending Maryland sensitive-places carry restrictions in Kipke v. Moore / Novotny v. Moore; OAG actively defends DC firearms-on-transit ban.

1.note: OAG defends DC's prohibition on large-capacity magazines in active 2A litigation; co-led 24-AG amicus in Garland v. VanDerStok (ghost-gun rule, 7-2 upheld March 26 2025).

profile.background: DC Council Ward 1 since 2015; chairs Committee on Human Services (oversight of Mayor's Office of LGBTQ+ Affairs). LAME DUCK: announced Sept 25 2025 will NOT seek 4th term — serves through Jan 2 2027. Successor primary underway (Brian Footer and others). Did NOT lose the 2022 or 2024 primary — defeated Salah Czapary 48.5% to 30.9% in June 21 2022.

brianne-nadeau.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active LAME-DUCK (Sept 25 2025 retirement announcement). Bottom-decile progressive profile. HARD-FALSE Pillar A: (life q0/q1) AUTHOR of Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act 2022 making DC an abortion 'sanctuary' shielding self-managed abortion and out-of-state providers; (marriage q0) chairs committee overseeing Mayor's Office of LGBTQ+ Affairs, champions $1M annual LGBTQ grant funding; (marriage q2) SPONSORED permanent non-binary 'X' gender marker on DC IDs (2018, enacted) + Human Rights Sanctuary expressly protects 'gender-affirmation care' incl. trans youth. HARD-FALSE Pillar B: (election q1) AUTHOR of Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 allowing noncitizens incl. undocumented to vote in DC local elections — passed Council 12-0-1; (public_justice q0) 2020 endorsed 'calls to defund MPD' + 'get police out of schools', voted to cut $15M from MPD budget; (public_justice q2) voted YES RCCA + 12-1 Jan 2023 veto override (lower penalties for violent/gun offenses), called Bowser veto 'political theater'; (border q1) Author of Human Rights Sanctuary 2022 + signed Local Progress 100+ Mayors sanctuary pledge 2017; (border q2) wrote DC's permanent immigrant-legal-services fund law; (self_defense q0) 'obviously outrageous' re Congressional proposals to roll back DC concealed-carry restrictions; (self_defense q1) authored DC red-flag (ERPO) law + co-introduced NEAR Act. TWO cross-pressure TRUEs under recall threat: (public_justice q1 HARD-TRUE) voted YES Secure DC Omnibus Mar 5 2024 — drug-free zones, expanded penalties; (public_justice q4 SOFT-TRUE) authored Secure DC witness-assistance evaluation amendment. SOFT-FALSE: (christian_liberty q0) Human Rights Sanctuary creates private right of action against parties bringing claims related to abortion/LGBTQ conduct → narrows conscience-based civil action — left null in dossier per high-bar discipline (chilling-effect SOFT, not own-statement); (econ q2) created permanent Tax & Revenue Commission to 'broaden tax base', consistently expanded housing-voucher/homelessness spending → marked FALSE SOFT; (public_justice q3) publicly defended DC cashless-bail framework vs Trump 2025 EO. NULL: marriage q4 (no specific cited vote, but record uniformly LGBTQ-affirming); family_child_sovereignty all 3 (no specific cited record); christian_liberty q2/q3/q4; election q0/q4; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17).

0.note: Lead sponsor of Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act of 2022 making DC an abortion sanctuary.

1.note: Same Human Rights Sanctuary bill — codifies shielding abortion providers from out-of-state enforcement.

0.note: Chairs the committee with oversight of Mayor's Office of LGBTQ+ Affairs; champions $1M annual LGBTQ grant funding.

2.note: Sponsored permanent non-binary 'X' gender marker on DC IDs (2018, enacted); Human Rights Sanctuary expressly protects 'gender-affirmation' care including for trans youth.
Created permanent Tax & Revenue Commission to 'broaden tax base'; consistently expanded housing-voucher / homelessness spending.

1.note: AUTHOR of Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 allowing noncitizens incl. undocumented to vote in DC local elections — passed Council 12-0-1.

0.note: 2020 endorsed 'calls to defund MPD' + 'get police out of schools'; voted to cut $15M from MPD budget.

1.note: CROSS-PRESSURE under recall threat: voted YES on Secure DC Omnibus Amendment Act Mar 5 2024 — drug-free zones, expanded penalties, pretrial detention.

2.note: Voted YES Revised Criminal Code Act 2022 (lower penalties for violent/gun offenses); voted YES on 12-1 Jan 2023 Bowser-veto override, called veto 'political theater.'

3.note: Publicly defended DC's cashless-bail framework against Trump 2025 executive order.

4.note: Authored Secure DC amendment to evaluate witness-assistance programs (passed unanimously Mar 2024) — victim/witness protection.

1.note: Author of Human Rights Sanctuary Amendment Act 2022; signed Local Progress 100+ Mayors sanctuary pledge 2017.

2.note: Wrote DC's law establishing permanent immigrant-legal-services fund.

0.note: 'Opposed proposals from Members of Congress to roll back restrictions on DC's concealed carry prohibition, calling this obviously outrageous.'

1.note: Authored DC red-flag (ERPO) law; co-introduced NEAR Act.

profile.background: DC Council Ward 2 since June 27 2020. Chair, Committee on the Judiciary and Public Safety (since Dec 21 2022). Announced Oct 6 2025 as candidate for DC Delegate to Congress (2026).

brooke-pinto.notes_append: [2026-06-05 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified active (brookepintodc.com). HEADLINE CROSS-PARTY CATCH: D Judiciary chair with a public-safety-pragmatist record that earns FOUR HARD-TRUE public_justice cells against a uniformly progressive social-issue voting record. HARD-TRUE Pillar B public-justice cluster: (q0) authored Secure DC Omnibus (drug-free zones, mask ban, neck restraints, high-speed chases) — affirmative pro-LE program; (q1) Secure DC creates rebuttable presumption in favor of pretrial detention for juveniles charged with violent crimes; (q2) FY26 Judiciary budget funded ALL of MPD's request + $2.5M training-academy capital + $2.4M Police & Fire Clinic + $2.3M 911/311 raises; Peace DC plan explicitly increases MPD/DOC/DYRS hiring; (q3) was 3-vote minority pushing HIGHER gun-penalty maxes during RCCA process: 'important that judges have more discretion for these penalties'; (q4) Secure DC makes permanent felony of endangerment-with-a-firearm, prohibits gun possession by stalking convictees, raises max gun-crime sentences 1→5 years. TRUE-SOFT econ q2: publicly opposed BOTH FY25 employer payroll-tax increase AND residential property-tax increase on homes >$2.5M ('neither of which Pinto supported'), though still voted final budget package. HARD-FALSE social cluster: (life q0) co-introduced 4 bills shielding/expanding abortion post-Dobbs incl. Enhancing Reproductive Health Protections + insurer cost-sharing mandate; (marriage q0) co-introduced William Dorsey Swann Street Designation Act 2023 + funded $1M LGBTQQIA+ Community Center + $479K LGBTQ senior housing vouchers; (marriage q2) explicit support for 'trans women of color' protections, Bella Evangelista/Tony Hunter Panic Defense Prohibition Act 2020; (election q1) CO-INTRODUCED Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 allowing noncitizens (incl. undocumented) to vote — 'true representation requires that all of our residents' voices are heard'; (self_defense q0) authored expansion of DC firearm offenses + increased penalties + new crime for firing gun in public; (self_defense q1) voted YES Nov 16 2021 DC ghost-gun definition expansion + publicly advocates 'tamping down ghost guns.' SOFT-FALSE: (border q1) defended DC sanctuary status vs House disapproval 'trample our right to Home Rule' but did NOT author original Sanctuary Values Act (predated her chairmanship); (christian_liberty q2) led 2021 'COVID Recovery and Vaccination Plan' briefing — supportive of mandates regime, no opposition recorded → left NULL in dossier per high-bar discipline. NULL: life q1; marriage q4; family_child_sovereignty all 3; christian_liberty all 4; election q0/q4; border q2; refuse_state_overreach all 5 (DC has no state-overreach axis — Home Rule disputes are with Congress under Art. I §8 cl. 17). Net profile: PUBLIC-SAFETY-PRAGMATIST DEMOCRAT with progressive social-issue priors and one HARD-TRUE fiscal cross-pressure — the cleanest cross-party catch in this batch.

0.note: Co-introduced four bills shielding/expanding abortion post-Dobbs incl. Enhancing Reproductive Health Protections Amendment Act + required private insurers cover abortion w/o cost-sharing; stood with Planned Parenthood (May 2022).
Co-introduced William Dorsey Swann Street Designation Act 2023 commemorating LGBTQ-rights pioneer; funded $1M LGBTQQIA+ Community Center + $479K LGBTQ senior housing vouchers.

2.note: Explicit support for 'trans women of color' protections; funded LGBTQ youth shelter; supported Bella Evangelista/Tony Hunter Panic Defense Prohibition Act 2020.

CROSS-PRESSURE SOFT: publicly opposed FY25 employer payroll-tax increase AND residential property-tax increase on homes >$2.5M — 'two notable tax increases in the budget, neither of which Pinto supported.' (Still voted final budget package — softness flag.)

1.note: CO-INTRODUCED Local Resident Voting Rights Amendment Act of 2022 allowing noncitizens (incl. green-card holders AND those without legal status) to vote in DC local elections after 30 days residency. Quoted: 'True representation requires that all of our residents' voices are heard.'

0.note: HIGH-BAR met: authored Secure DC Omnibus (drug-free zones, mask ban, neck restraints, high-speed chases) — affirmative pro-MPD program, not routine budget.

1.note: HEADLINE CROSS-PARTY CATCH: Secure DC creates rebuttable presumption in favor of pretrial detention for juveniles charged with violent crimes. Pinto authored.

2.note: FY26 Judiciary budget funded ALL of MPD's request + $2.5M training-academy capital + $2.4M Police & Fire Clinic + $2.3M for 911/311 staff raises; Peace DC plan increases MPD/DOC/DYRS hiring.

3.note: Moved amendment to INCREASE max penalties for illegal gun possession during RCCA process; struck down 10-3 with Pinto in pro-penalty minority. 'Important that judges have more discretion for these penalties.'

4.note: Secure DC makes permanent felony of endangerment-with-a-firearm (shooting into a crowd even if no one hit); prohibits gun possession by stalking convictees; raises max sentence for some gun crimes 1→5 years; new offense for firing a gun in public.

1.note: Opposed House-passed bills ending DC sanctuary status ('trample our right to Home Rule'); did NOT author original Sanctuary Values Act 2020 but defended sanctuary posture publicly.

0.note: Authored expansion of DC firearm offenses, increased penalties for gun possession, new crime for firing gun in public.

1.note: Voted YES on DC ghost-gun definition expansion Nov 16 2021; publicly advocates 'tamp down on the use of ghost guns.'
